SEARCH
Package com.cellsimulation.model

Class SimulationSettings

java.lang.Object
com.cellsimulation.model.SimulationSettings
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class SimulationSettings extends Object implements Serializable
Centralizes the nine tunable parameters of the simulation.

Both the SimulationEngine and the Person instances read their configuration from a single SimulationSettings instance, so that probabilities, durations and speed remain consistent across the whole simulation.

The nine parameters are:

  • simulationSpeed - ticks per second.
  • transmissionProbability - chance of contaminating a susceptible neighbor per tick.
  • recoveryProbability - chance of recovering naturally per tick.
  • mortalityProbability - chance of dying from the disease per tick.
  • mobilityRate - chance of attempting a move per tick.
  • maxInfectionDays - hard cap of infection duration after which the person recovers automatically.
  • meanImmunity - mean of the immunity factor drawn for each new person.
  • immunityVariance - spread of the truncated-normal distribution used to draw each immunity factor around meanImmunity.
  • vaccineEfficacy - minimum immunity granted to a vaccinated person.

This class is Serializable so that it can be embedded in a serialized SimulationEngine when the user saves the simulation state to disk.

See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SimulationSettings()
    Creates a new SimulationSettings populated with the default values: simulationSpeed = 1 transmissionProbability = 0.30 recoveryProbability = 0.05 mortalityProbability = 0.01 mobilityRate = 0.20 maxInfectionDays = 14 meanImmunity = 0.0 immunityVariance = 0.0 vaccineEfficacy = 0.85

  • Constructor Details

    • SimulationSettings

      public SimulationSettings()
      Creates a new SimulationSettings populated with the default values:
      • simulationSpeed = 1
      • transmissionProbability = 0.30
      • recoveryProbability = 0.05
      • mortalityProbability = 0.01
      • mobilityRate = 0.20
      • maxInfectionDays = 14
      • meanImmunity = 0.0
      • immunityVariance = 0.0
      • vaccineEfficacy = 0.85

  • Method Details

    • getSimulationSpeed

      public int getSimulationSpeed()
      Returns:
      the simulation speed, in ticks per second

    • getTransmissionProbability

      public double getTransmissionProbability()
      Returns:
      the probability of transmission to a susceptible neighbor per tick

    • getRecoveryProbability

      public double getRecoveryProbability()
      Returns:
      the probability of natural recovery per tick

    • getMortalityProbability

      public double getMortalityProbability()
      Returns:
      the probability of dying from the disease per tick

    • getMobilityRate

      public double getMobilityRate()
      Returns:
      the probability of attempting a move per tick

    • getMaxInfectionDays

      public int getMaxInfectionDays()
      Returns:
      the maximum number of ticks a person stays infected before being forcibly recovered

    • setSimulationSpeed

      public void setSimulationSpeed(int simulationSpeed)
      Sets the simulation speed.
      Parameters:
      simulationSpeed - the number of ticks per second; must be greater than or equal to 1
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if simulationSpeed < 1

    • setTransmissionProbability

      public void setTransmissionProbability(double transmissionProbability)
      Sets the transmission probability.
      Parameters:
      transmissionProbability - the probability, in [0.0, 1.0]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 1.0]

    • setRecoveryProbability

      public void setRecoveryProbability(double recoveryProbability)
      Sets the recovery probability.
      Parameters:
      recoveryProbability - the probability, in [0.0, 1.0]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 1.0]

    • setMortalityProbability

      public void setMortalityProbability(double mortalityProbability)
      Sets the mortality probability.
      Parameters:
      mortalityProbability - the probability, in [0.0, 1.0]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 1.0]

    • setMobilityRate

      public void setMobilityRate(double mobilityRate)
      Sets the mobility rate.
      Parameters:
      mobilityRate - the probability, in [0.0, 1.0], of attempting a move per tick
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 1.0]

    • setMaxInfectionDays

      public void setMaxInfectionDays(int maxInfectionDays)
      Sets the maximum infection duration.
      Parameters:
      maxInfectionDays - the cap, in ticks; must be greater than or equal to 1
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxInfectionDays < 1

    • getMeanImmunity

      public double getMeanImmunity()
      Returns:
      the mean immunity factor drawn for each new person

    • setMeanImmunity

      public void setMeanImmunity(double meanImmunity)
      Sets the mean immunity factor used when drawing the immunity of each new person.
      Parameters:
      meanImmunity - the mean immunity, in [0.0, 1.0]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 1.0]

    • getImmunityVariance

      public double getImmunityVariance()
      Returns:
      the spread of the truncated-normal distribution used to draw each immunity factor

    • setImmunityVariance

      public void setImmunityVariance(double immunityVariance)
      Sets the spread of the truncated-normal distribution used to draw each immunity factor.

      The value is capped at 0.5 because beyond that the truncated normal collapses against the [0.0, 1.0] bounds and loses its meaning.

      Parameters:
      immunityVariance - the spread, in [0.0, 0.5]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 0.5]

    • getVaccineEfficacy

      public double getVaccineEfficacy()
      Returns:
      the minimum immunity granted to a vaccinated person

    • setVaccineEfficacy

      public void setVaccineEfficacy(double vaccineEfficacy)
      Sets the vaccine efficacy, i.e. the minimum immunity granted to a vaccinated person.
      Parameters:
      vaccineEfficacy - the efficacy, in [0.0, 1.0]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is outside [0.0, 1.0]
